Use CasesLaw IIIF5Enterprise SLA Breach Prevention
Law III: Service StratificationF5: SLA Verificationsolo

Enterprise SLA Breach Prevention

Trigger

Active probe latency on enterprise slice exceeds 80% of contracted SLA threshold for 15 continuous minutes.

Tools Required

Emblasoft EvolverEmblasoft

Response at Each Autonomy Level

L1

NOC engineer alerted. Manual resource reallocation within 30 minutes.

L2

Automated QoS adjustment within slice parameters. Engineer notified.

L3

Cross-domain resource reallocation. SLA clock paused pending resolution. Customer portal updated.

L4

Network autonomously reallocates resources, prevents breach, and logs compliance evidence — before SLA clock would have triggered.

Economic Outcome

SLA breach penalty prevented. Enterprise contract renewal protected.

Typical enterprise SLA breach penalty: 10–20% monthly contract value. For a €50K/month enterprise contract: €5K–€10K per breach. At 12 potential breaches/year: €60K–€120K penalty avoidance.

Combination Upgrade

Add Emblasoft nScan (passive probe) to identify which enterprise users were affected within the slice — enabling precise SLA evidence and impact scoping.

Related Use Cases

Law IIIF7solo

Revenue Leakage Identification

Passive probe CDR correlation identifies 3% of data sessions with no corresponding billing record over 30-day audit window.

L1L2L3L4
Unbilled data sessions identified and recovered. Systematic billing gap closed.
